title: Nanotechnology
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Nanotechnology is (0) the science of manipulating matter at the atomic and molecular scale, typically working with structures between 1 and 100 nanometres. To put this in perspective, a nanometre is one billionth of a metre – a human hair is approximately 80,000 nanometres wide.||At this tiny scale, materials can behave very differently (9) from how they behave in bulk. Properties such as colour, strength, and electrical conductivity can change dramatically. Scientists are exploiting these unique properties (10) to develop innovative products across numerous fields, from medicine to electronics. For example, carbon nanotubes are incredibly strong yet lightweight, making (11) them ideal for applications in aerospace and construction.||In medicine, nanotechnology offers exciting possibilities. Nanoparticles can (12) be designed to deliver drugs directly to cancer cells, reducing side effects by targeting treatment more precisely. Researchers are also developing nanosensors that could/might/may (13) detect diseases at much earlier stages than current methods allow.||Nanotechnology, (14) which has revolutionised the electronics industry, makes it possible to produce smaller and more powerful computer chips using nanoscale manufacturing techniques. However, there (15) are concerns about potential health and environmental risks of nanomaterials, as their small size allows them to enter the body easily. More research is needed to fully understand these risks (16) before nanotechnology products become widespread in consumer goods.

example.text: Nanotechnology is (0) science of manipulating matter at the atomic scale.

questions.explanation: Comparison 'differently from' indicates contrast with normal behaviour.
Expression 'exploiting properties to develop' with a purpose infinitive.
Object pronoun referring to carbon nanotubes.
Voz pasiva modal: 'can be designed' expresa posibilidad.
Relative pronoun used to introduce a non-restrictive relative clause giving additional information about 'Nanotechnology'.
Simple present: 'there are concerns' with a plural subject.
Temporal conjunction 'before' indicates priority to the spread.
